evals.name: blueprint-figjam
evals.prompt:
I just finished a discovery call with Bright Smile Dental, a dental clinic in Sydney. Here are the key points:

- They're a 3-dentist practice in Parramatta, open Mon-Sat 8am-6pm
- Currently missing 30% of inbound calls during peak hours (10am-2pm)
- Receptionist is overwhelmed — can't answer phones and handle walk-ins simultaneously
- They want an AI receptionist to handle: appointment booking, rescheduling, general inquiries (hours, location, services), and after-hours calls
- They use Google Calendar for scheduling and have a basic website
- Budget is flexible but they want to see ROI within 3 months
- Key contact: Dr. Sarah Chen (owner) and Maria (office manager)
- They mentioned competitors are using chatbots but they want voice specifically
- Insurance questions come up a lot — they accept most major providers
- They need the AI to sound warm and professional, not robotic

Create a blueprint for this project.
evals.expected_output: A structured blueprint document with phases AND a FigJam flowchart created via generate_diagram MCP tool (not Mermaid fallback)

assertions.name: has_discovery_summary
assertions.description: Blueprint contains a discovery summary with pain points and goals extracted from the brief

assertions.name: has_phase_breakdown
assertions.description: Blueprint contains numbered phases with sub-tasks

assertions.name: uses_figjam_mcp
assertions.description: Called generate_diagram or mcp__claude_ai_Figma__generate_diagram to create the flowchart, NOT defaulting to Mermaid

assertions.name: no_mermaid_default
assertions.description: Did not output a Mermaid diagram as the primary flowchart (Mermaid only acceptable if FigJam explicitly errored)

evals.name: prototype-step0
evals.prompt: I have the blueprint and call flowchart ready for Bright Smile Dental. The blueprint says it's an inbound receptionist agent on Vapi. Build the prototype agent for me.
evals.expected_output: Agent should ask to confirm platform (Vapi) and direction (inbound) BEFORE proceeding, even though the blueprint mentions it. Should then produce system prompt as .docx and demo instructions as .docx.

assertions.name: asks_platform_confirmation
assertions.description: Explicitly asks the user to confirm platform (Vapi/Livekit) before building

assertions.name: asks_direction_confirmation
assertions.description: Explicitly asks the user to confirm direction (inbound/outbound/both) before building

assertions.name: does_not_skip_step0
assertions.description: Does NOT immediately start building without asking — waits for user confirmation

assertions.name: mentions_docx_output
assertions.description: References .docx output for system prompt and/or demo instructions

assertions.name: references_guardrails
assertions.description: References the Guardrails section or 35 non-negotiable rules when discussing system prompt

evals.name: reports-docx
evals.prompt:
Here are call summaries from last week for the Bright Smile Dental AI receptionist agent:

Call 1: Caller booked appointment for Thursday 2pm. Duration: 2m 15s. Sentiment: positive.
Call 2: Caller asked about insurance (Bupa). Agent confirmed they accept Bupa. Booked for Monday 10am. Duration: 3m 40s. Sentiment: positive.
Call 3: Angry caller — waited on hold previously. Agent de-escalated, offered callback. Duration: 4m 10s. Sentiment: started negative, ended neutral.
Call 4: Wrong number — caller wanted pizza delivery. Agent handled gracefully. Duration: 30s. Sentiment: neutral.
Call 5: After-hours call. Agent informed of hours, offered to book for next day. Booked for Tuesday 9am. Duration: 1m 50s. Sentiment: positive.
Call 6: Caller asked about teeth whitening pricing. Agent said it varies and offered to book a consult. Duration: 2m 20s. Sentiment: positive.
Call 7: Caller wanted to reschedule from Wednesday to Friday. Agent checked calendar and confirmed. Duration: 1m 30s. Sentiment: positive.
Call 8: Caller asked 'Are you a robot?' Agent confirmed AI, caller laughed and proceeded to book. Duration: 3m. Sentiment: positive.
Call 9: Caller spoke very slowly, lots of pauses. Agent stayed patient. Booked for Thursday 11am. Duration: 5m 20s. Sentiment: positive.
Call 10: Caller asked for Dr. Chen's personal mobile number. Agent declined appropriately and offered to transfer. Duration: 1m 10s. Sentiment: neutral.

Create a QA report for February 2026.
evals.expected_output: A QA report generated as .docx using python-docx, with scoring rubric applied, flagged calls identified, and professional formatting

assertions.name: generates_docx
assertions.description: Generates a .docx file using python-docx (not markdown output)

assertions.name: has_scoring
assertions.description: Includes dimension scores (Greeting, Listening, Accuracy, Tone, Flow, Compliance, Resolution)

assertions.name: flags_problematic_calls
assertions.description: Identifies and flags calls that need attention (e.g., Call 3 angry caller, Call 10 data request)

assertions.name: has_recommendations
assertions.description: Includes actionable recommendations based on the call data

assertions.name: professional_format
assertions.description: Uses proper formatting: title, headings, tables, not raw markdown
